{"key_event":"1853 Constitution and federalization","reasoning":"Broad male suffrage, partisan press, clubs, artisan societies, emancipation, and provincial autonomy expanded organized political participation, though the defeat of Melo and weakening of some corporate communities kept society short of strong. Central authority could still defeat revolts and assert itself against the Church, but fiscal liberalization and the 1853-1858 move toward confederation left national state capacity weaker and more dependent on regional powerholders.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":5.8,"state_change":-0.4,"state_power":3.2}
